steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The student claims irrigation is humanity's principal use of water. To support that claim, we need a quotation that compares irrigation to other water uses and shows irrigation comes out on top.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — “The first known irrigation system was built in the 7th century CE by the Hohokam people, a Native population in the American Southwest that inhabited central Arizona.”: Describes the origin of irrigation in one region. Says nothing about how irrigation compares to other uses of water globally. ✗
  • Choice B — “Roughly 70% of the world's water withdrawals, from sources such as rivers, lakes, and aquifers, is for irrigation water used to meet various agricultural demands.”: Directly establishes that irrigation accounts for 70% of all water withdrawals worldwide, making it humanity's principal use. ✓
  • Choice C — “Today, many areas of the southwestern United States utilize sprinkler irrigation systems, which require machinery that can spray water in all directions.”: Describes a type of irrigation technology. Makes no comparison to other water uses and gives no sense of scale. ✗
  • Choice D — “The irrigation system developed by the Hohokam people in the 7th century CE in what is now known as central Arizona was simple but applied hydraulic engineering design features that are still used today.”: Focuses on the longevity of one ancient system's techniques. No comparison to other water uses. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
